title: bridge_health parallelize Ω-cycle

profile.bottleneck: (a) per-bridge gtimeout 30 hexa run --selftest serialised at ~2s avg (b) uniprot 12s tail (single-bridge critical-path floor) (c) hexa runtime per-call cold start

strategy_choice.rationale: consistency with falsifier_health_parallel.sh (same OPT-D pattern); stdlib-only; clean output ordering by registry index; ProcessPoolExecutor handles ec/timeout/exc uniformly

OPT_D_impl.design: bash wrapper materialises temp .py worker; worker fans out classify(name, rel) across N workers via ProcessPoolExecutor
per_bridge_work: R1 SHA256 16-hex check vs state/bridge_sha256.tsv (skip if no decl)
subprocess.run([gtimeout, 30, hexa, run, script, --selftest]) with HEXA_RESOLVER_NO_REROUTE=1
classify PASS / FAIL / TAMPERED / OFFLINE via sentinel regex + fallback regex

equivalence.test: run sequential + parallel on full 16-bridge set; diff per-bridge status + sentinel fields

docker_bottleneck_investigation.concern_raised_by_task_brief: hexa runtime runs in docker container hexa-exec; concurrent invocations may serialise at container level
docker_bottleneck_investigation.finding: bridge_health.sh sets HEXA_RESOLVER_NO_REROUTE=1, which short-circuits the resolver's docker-exec round-trip (~/.hx/bin/hexa) and uses build/hexa.real native dispatcher directly — each subprocess is an independent native Mach-O process
docker_bottleneck_investigation.implication: hexa-exec container is NOT in the per-bridge call path; no docker serialisation
docker_bottleneck_investigation.verdict: matches caution prediction — limited by uniprot 12s tail (Amdahl), not docker serialisation
docker_bottleneck_investigation.amdahl_analysis: max theoretical speedup with 12s critical-path tail = 36/12 = 3.0x; achieved 2.43x at -j 8 = 81% of theoretical
docker_bottleneck_investigation.follow_up: if more speedup needed, investigate uniprot bridge selftest cold-start (12s vs 0-3s for others)

tier_1_outcomes: equivalence verified — all 16 bridges PASS in both seq and par; sentinel fields match (total/pass/fail/tampered/offline_fallback)
health_check_all.sh integrated via BRIDGE_HEALTH_TOOL env (default = parallel)
docker bottleneck honestly investigated — N/A because HEXA_RESOLVER_NO_REROUTE=1 bypasses container; Amdahl tail (uniprot 12s) is the real ceiling
speedup 2.31x default / 2.43x at -j 8 — modest as predicted by task brief caution

fixpoint.marker: bridge_health parallelize Ω-cycle reaches fixed point: parallel tool live, equivalent to sequential, integrated, witness sealed
next_cycle_candidates: uniprot bridge selftest cold-start investigation (12s tail dominates Amdahl)
extend OPT-D pattern to atlas_status_all.sh if further parallelism needed
consider per-bridge timeout tightening (default 30s but max observed 13s; could drop to 20s)

non_goals: do not modify sequential bridge_health.sh — kept as canonical fallback
do not commit (main thread batches per task brief)
do not add new bridges (raw 77 read-only registry)
